authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Kidd Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Kidd Lake not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Kidd Lake not listed among Placer County waters)
Kidd & Cascade Lakes Preserve — Placer Land Trust
sources.quotedText: The property is owned and managed by Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E) and leased by Royal Gorge XC Ski Resort for cross country skiing and snowshoeing. Protected in 2017 by Conservation Easement granted by Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

sources.title: Kidd Lake Group Campground — California's Best Camping
sources.quotedText: Recreation at the ½-mile long lake includes non-motorized boating and swimming. During the summer water levels at Kidd Lake can fluctuate as water is drawn down for hydroelectric needs. The campground is operated by PG&E.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Reservoirs/lakes layer record for gaz_id 226570, "Kidd Lake", Reservoir, CA, Placer Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":226570,"gaz_name":"Kidd Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Reservoir","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Placer","objectid_1":1796013}. Geometry x=-13405964.049, y=4766145.258 (Web Mercator), converting to 39.3098°N, 120.4278°W at the lake itself. Corrects prior lon/lat -120.4216/39.3124, which matched the Kidd Lake Group Campground's coordinates (per topozone.com's separate 'Kidd Lake Campgrond' locale page) rather than the reservoir feature, a ~610m discrepancy.

parameters.note: Kidd Lake (Placer Co.), a small PG&E hydroelectric impoundment on the Old Highway 40 corridor near Soda Springs/Serene Lakes, is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1), which applies to all inland lakes and reservoirs 'including those on private lands.'

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Third-party recreation guide describes Kidd Lake as limited to non-motorized boating; this is not independently confirmed on PG&E's own recreation site, so treat as informational rather than a confirmed regulatory prohibition.

regulations.citation: California's Best Camping — Kidd Lake Group Campground
regulations.humanSummary: Reported as limited to non-motorized boating (small PG&E reservoir, per third-party recreation guide; not confirmed directly on PG&E's own site)
regulations.verbatimText: Recreation at the ½-mile long lake includes non-motorized boating and swimming.
